sentences.zh: 夏禹，名曰文命。

translations.literal: Xia's Yu was called Wenming.
translations.idiomatic: Yu of Xia bore the name Wenming.

sentences.zh: 禹之父曰鯀，鯀之父曰帝顓頊，顓頊之父曰昌意，昌意之父曰黃帝。

translations.literal: Yu's father was called Gun; Gun's father was called Emperor Zhuanxu; Zhuanxu's father was called Changyi; Changyi's father was the Yellow Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Yu's father was Gun; Gun's father was Emperor Zhuanxu; Zhuanxu's father was Changyi; Changyi's father was the Yellow Emperor.

sentences.zh: 禹者，黃帝之玄孫而帝顓頊之孫也。

translations.literal: Yu was the Yellow Emperor's great-great-grandson and Emperor Zhuanxu's grandson.
translations.idiomatic: Yu was a great-great-grandson of the Yellow Emperor and a grandson of Emperor Zhuanxu.

sentences.zh: 禹之曾大父昌意及父鯀皆不得在帝位，為人臣。

translations.literal: Yu's great-grandfather Changyi and father Gun both did not attain the imperial throne; they were subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Neither Yu's great-grandfather Changyi nor his father Gun ever held the throne; both served merely as ministers.

translations.literal: Xia's Yu was called Wenming. Yu's father was called Gun; Gun's father was called Emperor Zhuanxu; Zhuanxu's father was called Changyi; Changyi's father was the Yellow Emperor. Yu was the Yellow Emperor's great-great-grandson and Emperor Zhuanxu's grandson. Yu's great-grandfather Changyi and father Gun both did not attain the imperial throne; they were subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Yu of Xia bore the name Wenming. Yu's father was Gun; Gun's father was Emperor Zhuanxu; Zhuanxu's father was Changyi; Changyi's father was the Yellow Emperor. Yu was a great-great-grandson of the Yellow Emperor and a grandson of Emperor Zhuanxu. Neither Yu's great-grandfather Changyi nor his father Gun ever held the throne; both served merely as ministers.

sentences.zh: 當帝堯之時，鴻水滔天，浩浩懷山襄陵，下民其憂。

translations.literal: In Emperor Yao's time the vast flood swelled to heaven; vastly it embraced mountains and overflowed hills; the common people worried over it.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Yao the floodwaters rose to the sky, spread around the peaks, and overtopped the hills, and the people lived in dread of them.

sentences.zh: 堯求能治水者，群臣四嶽皆曰鯀可。

translations.literal: Yao sought one able to regulate the waters; all ministers and presidents of the Four Peaks said Gun could do it.
translations.idiomatic: Yao looked for someone who could manage the flood; the officials and the lords of the Four Peaks all named Gun.

sentences.zh: 堯曰：「鯀為人負命毀族，不可。」

translations.literal: Yao said, "Gun as a man defies commands and ruins kin groups; not acceptable."
translations.idiomatic: Yao replied, "Gun disobeys orders and harms his own people. I cannot use him."

sentences.zh: 四嶽曰：「等之未有賢於鯀者，願帝試之。」

translations.literal: The Four Peaks said, "Among their peers none is worthier than Gun; we beg the Emperor to try him."
translations.idiomatic: The Four Peaks answered, "No one in their ranks surpasses Gun; we ask you to give him a trial."

sentences.zh: 於是堯聽四嶽，用鯀治水。

translations.literal: Thereupon Yao heeded the Four Peaks and employed Gun to regulate the waters.
translations.idiomatic: Yao yielded and put Gun in charge of the flood works.

sentences.zh: 九年而水不息，功用不成。

translations.literal: After nine years still the waters did not subside; the task was not completed.
translations.idiomatic: Nine years passed, the flood never relented, and the project failed.

sentences.zh: 於是帝堯乃求人，更得舜。

translations.literal: Thereupon Emperor Yao then sought another man and obtained Shun in his stead.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Yao began looking again and found Shun.

sentences.zh: 舜登用，攝行天子之政，巡狩。

translations.literal: Shun was promoted and employed; he exercised the Son of Heaven's government in proxy and went on tours of inspection.
translations.idiomatic: Shun was raised to high office, administered affairs in the Son of Heaven's name, and toured the realm.

sentences.zh: 行視鯀之治水無狀，乃殛鯀於羽山以死。

translations.literal: On tour he observed Gun's regulation of the waters—no achievement—and then struck Gun dead on Mount Yu.
translations.idiomatic: On his rounds he saw that Gun had achieved nothing with the waters, so he had Gun executed on Mount Yu.

sentences.zh: 天下皆以舜之誅為是。

translations.literal: All under Heaven considered Shun's punishment right.
translations.idiomatic: Throughout the realm people judged Shun's sentence just.

sentences.zh: 於是舜舉鯀子禹，而使續鯀之業。

translations.literal: Thereupon Shun raised Gun's son Yu and had him continue Gun's task.
translations.idiomatic: Shun then promoted Gun's son Yu to carry on his father's work.

translations.literal: In Emperor Yao's time the vast flood swelled to heaven; vastly it embraced mountains and overflowed hills; the common people worried over it. Yao sought one able to regulate the waters; all ministers and presidents of the Four Peaks said Gun could do it. Yao said, "Gun as a man defies commands and ruins kin groups; not acceptable." The Four Peaks said, "Among their peers none is worthier than Gun; we beg the Emperor to try him." Thereupon Yao heeded the Four Peaks and employed Gun to regulate the waters. After nine years still the waters did not subside; the task was not completed. Thereupon Emperor Yao then sought another man and obtained Shun in his stead. Shun was promoted and employed; he exercised the Son of Heaven's government in proxy and went on tours of inspection. On tour he observed Gun's regulation of the waters—no achievement—and then struck Gun dead on Mount Yu. All under Heaven considered Shun's punishment right. Thereupon Shun raised Gun's son Yu and had him continue Gun's task.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Yao the floodwaters rose to the sky, spread around the peaks, and overtopped the hills, and the people lived in dread of them. Yao looked for someone who could manage the flood; the officials and the lords of the Four Peaks all named Gun. Yao replied, "Gun disobeys orders and harms his own people. I cannot use him." The Four Peaks answered, "No one in their ranks surpasses Gun; we ask you to give him a trial." Yao yielded and put Gun in charge of the flood works. Nine years passed, the flood never relented, and the project failed. Emperor Yao began looking again and found Shun. Shun was raised to high office, administered affairs in the Son of Heaven's name, and toured the realm. On his rounds he saw that Gun had achieved nothing with the waters, so he had Gun executed on Mount Yu. Throughout the realm people judged Shun's sentence just. Shun then promoted Gun's son Yu to carry on his father's work.

sentences.zh: 堯崩，帝舜問四嶽曰：「有能成美堯之事者使居官？」

translations.literal: Yao passed away; Emperor Shun questioned the Four Peaks, saying, "Is there anyone who can complete and beautify Yao's undertakings—appoint him to office?"
translations.idiomatic: When Yao died, Emperor Shun asked the Four Peaks whether anyone could bring Yao's projects to fruition and hold office.

sentences.zh: 皆曰：「伯禹為司空，可成美堯之功。」

translations.literal: All said, "Lord Yu as Minister of Works can complete and beautify Yao's achievements."
translations.idiomatic: They said, "Lord Yu, as Minister of Works, could finish what Yao began."

sentences.zh: 舜曰：「嗟，然！」

translations.literal: Shun said, "Alas—yes!"
translations.idiomatic: Shun exclaimed, "Well said—it shall be so."

sentences.zh: 命禹：「女平水土，維是勉之。」

translations.literal: He charged Yu: "You—level the waters and earth; only this—exert yourself."
translations.idiomatic: He ordered Yu: "You shall bring the land and waters to order; give it your whole strength."

sentences.zh: 禹拜稽首，讓於契、后稷、皋陶。

translations.literal: Yu bowed touching head to ground and yielded to Xie, Prince Millet, and Gao Yao.
translations.idiomatic: Yu kowtowed and declined in favor of Xie, Hou Ji, and Gao Yao.

sentences.zh: 舜曰：「女其往視爾事矣。」

translations.literal: Shun said, "You—go tend your affairs."
translations.idiomatic: Shun told him, "Go—all that matters is that you do the work."

translations.literal: Yao passed away; Emperor Shun questioned the Four Peaks, saying, "Is there anyone who can complete and beautify Yao's undertakings—appoint him to office?" All said, "Lord Yu as Minister of Works can complete and beautify Yao's achievements." Shun said, "Alas—yes!" He charged Yu: "You—level the waters and earth; only this—exert yourself." Yu bowed touching head to ground and yielded to Xie, Prince Millet, and Gao Yao. Shun said, "You—go tend your affairs."
translations.idiomatic: When Yao died, Emperor Shun asked the Four Peaks whether anyone could bring Yao's projects to fruition and hold office. They said, "Lord Yu, as Minister of Works, could finish what Yao began." Shun exclaimed, "Well said—it shall be so." He ordered Yu: "You shall bring the land and waters to order; give it your whole strength." Yu kowtowed and declined in favor of Xie, Hou Ji, and Gao Yao. Shun told him, "Go—all that matters is that you do the work."

sentences.zh: 禹為人敏給克勤；

translations.literal: Yu as a person was quick, responsive, and able in diligence.
translations.idiomatic: Yu was quick-witted, responsive, and tirelessly industrious.

sentences.zh: 其德不違，其仁可親，其言可信；

translations.literal: His virtue did not deviate; his benevolence was approachable; his words were trustworthy.
translations.idiomatic: He kept to the moral path, drew others with kindness, and spoke so that men could rely on him.

sentences.zh: 聲為律，身為度，稱以出；

translations.literal: His voice was a pitch-pipe; his body was a measure; proportion issued from them.
translations.idiomatic: His voice matched the tuning tubes, his bearing matched the plumb line, and he carried himself with measured grace.

sentences.zh: 亹亹穆穆，為綱為紀。

translations.literal: Earnest, earnest; solemn, solemn—he was warp and he was woof.
translations.idiomatic: Steadfast and grave, he set the pattern others followed.

translations.literal: Yu as a person was quick, responsive, and able in diligence. His virtue did not deviate; his benevolence was approachable; his words were trustworthy. His voice was a pitch-pipe; his body was a measure; proportion issued from them. Earnest, earnest; solemn, solemn—he was warp and he was woof.
translations.idiomatic: Yu was quick-witted, responsive, and tirelessly industrious. He kept to the moral path, drew others with kindness, and spoke so that men could rely on him. His voice matched the tuning tubes, his bearing matched the plumb line, and he carried himself with measured grace. Steadfast and grave, he set the pattern others followed.

sentences.zh: 禹乃遂與益、后稷奉帝命，命諸侯百姓興人徒以傅土，行山表木，定高山大川。

translations.literal: Yu then together with Yi and Hou Ji received the Emperor's commands; he ordered the lords and commoners to raise work gangs to spread earth, marched along mountains marking trees, and fixed the high mountains and great rivers.
translations.idiomatic: Yu, with Yi and Hou Ji, carried out the Son of Heaven's orders, mobilized nobles and commoners to shift soil, blazed trails along ridges and marked timbers, and charted every major height and watercourse.

sentences.zh: 禹傷先人父鯀功之不成受誅，乃勞身焦思，居外十三年，過家門不敢入。

translations.literal: Yu grieved that his forebear and father Gun had not completed the work and suffered execution; thereupon he wore out his body and burned his thoughts, dwelt outside thirteen years, passed his gate without daring to enter.
translations.idiomatic: He mourned that Gun had died unfinished yet under sentence of death, so he drove himself until body and mind were spent, spent thirteen years away from home, and even passed his own door without stopping.

sentences.zh: 薄衣食，致孝于鬼神。

translations.literal: He made thin his clothing and food; he extended filial devotion toward ghosts and spirits.
translations.idiomatic: He lived plainly in dress and diet and showed the spirits the utmost reverence.

sentences.zh: 卑宮室，致費於溝淢。

translations.literal: He made low his palace chambers; he expended effort on ditches and channels.
translations.idiomatic: He kept his halls modest and poured labor into drains and canals.

sentences.zh: 陸行乘車，水行乘船，泥行乘橇，山行乘檋。

translations.literal: On land he rode in a carriage; on water he rode in a boat; in mud he rode a sledge; in mountains he rode a palanquin.
translations.idiomatic: He traveled by carriage on dry ground, by boat on water, by sledge in mire, and by litter in the hills.

sentences.zh: 左準繩，右規矩，載四時，以開九州，通九道，陂九澤，度九山。

translations.literal: Left the marking-cord and line, right the compass and square; he bore the four seasons to open the nine provinces, open the nine roads, bank the nine marshes, measure the nine mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Plumb line in his left hand, square in his right, he followed the seasons, opened the nine provinces, linked the nine highways, diked the nine marshes, and surveyed the nine ranges.

sentences.zh: 令益予眾庶稻，可種卑溼。

translations.literal: He ordered Yi to give the multitude rice, plantable in low damp places.
translations.idiomatic: He told Yi to distribute rice seed for the people to sow in the wet lowlands.

sentences.zh: 命后稷予眾庶難得之食。

translations.literal: He ordered Prince Millet to give the multitude hard-to-obtain food.
translations.idiomatic: He told Hou Ji to supply food where famine threatened.

sentences.zh: 食少，調有餘相給，以均諸侯。

translations.literal: When food was scarce, he adjusted surplus to mutual aid so as to equalize the lords.
translations.idiomatic: Where supplies ran short, he moved surplus from one region to another so that no domain starved alone.

sentences.zh: 禹乃行相地宜所有以貢，及山川之便利。

translations.literal: Yu then proceeded to survey each land for what it should offer as tribute and for the convenience of mountains and rivers.
translations.idiomatic: He then assessed what each region could yield in tribute and how mountains and rivers could serve transport.

translations.literal: Yu then together with Yi and Hou Ji received the Emperor's commands; he ordered the lords and commoners to raise work gangs to spread earth, marched along mountains marking trees, and fixed the high mountains and great rivers. Yu grieved that his forebear and father Gun had not completed the work and suffered execution; thereupon he wore out his body and burned his thoughts, dwelt outside thirteen years, passed his gate without daring to enter. He made thin his clothing and food; he extended filial devotion toward ghosts and spirits. He made low his palace chambers; he expended effort on ditches and channels. On land he rode in a carriage; on water he rode in a boat; in mud he rode a sledge; in mountains he rode a palanquin. Left the marking-cord and line, right the compass and square; he bore the four seasons to open the nine provinces, open the nine roads, bank the nine marshes, measure the nine mountains. He ordered Yi to give the multitude rice, plantable in low damp places. He ordered Prince Millet to give the multitude hard-to-obtain food. When food was scarce, he adjusted surplus to mutual aid so as to equalize the lords. Yu then proceeded to survey each land for what it should offer as tribute and for the convenience of mountains and rivers.
translations.idiomatic: Yu, with Yi and Hou Ji, carried out the Son of Heaven's orders, mobilized nobles and commoners to shift soil, blazed trails along ridges and marked timbers, and charted every major height and watercourse. He mourned that Gun had died unfinished yet under sentence of death, so he drove himself until body and mind were spent, spent thirteen years away from home, and even passed his own door without stopping. He lived plainly in dress and diet and showed the spirits the utmost reverence. He kept his halls modest and poured labor into drains and canals. He traveled by carriage on dry ground, by boat on water, by sledge in mire, and by litter in the hills. Plumb line in his left hand, square in his right, he followed the seasons, opened the nine provinces, linked the nine highways, diked the nine marshes, and surveyed the nine ranges. He told Yi to distribute rice seed for the people to sow in the wet lowlands. He told Hou Ji to supply food where famine threatened. Where supplies ran short, he moved surplus from one region to another so that no domain starved alone. He then assessed what each region could yield in tribute and how mountains and rivers could serve transport.

sentences.zh: 禹行自冀州始。

translations.literal: Yu's progress began from Jizhou.
translations.idiomatic: Yu began his survey in Jizhou.

sentences.zh: 冀州：既載壺口，治梁及岐。

translations.literal: Jizhou: already loaded at Hukou, regulated Liang and Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In Jizhou he finished work at Hukou and brought order to the Liang and Qi ranges.

sentences.zh: 既修太原，至于嶽陽。

translations.literal: Already repaired Taiyuan, reaching the south of Mount Yue.
translations.idiomatic: He restored Taiyuan and continued south of Mount Yue.

sentences.zh: 覃懷致功，至於衡漳。

translations.literal: Tanhuai achieved merit, reaching Heng and Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: He completed the Tanhuai basin and carried the work to the Heng and Zhang rivers.

sentences.zh: 其土白壤。

translations.literal: Its soil was white loam.
translations.idiomatic: The soil was pale, loose earth.

sentences.zh: 賦上上錯，田中中，常、衛既從，大陸既為。

translations.literal: Revenue highest of highest with admixture; fields middle of middle; Chang and Wei already followed; the great plain was formed.
translations.idiomatic: Its tax was rated top grade with some second-grade tracts; its fields were middling; the Chang and Wei ran true, and the great alluvial plain emerged.

sentences.zh: 鳥夷皮服。

translations.literal: Bird-barbarians offered furs.
translations.idiomatic: Coastal peoples sent furs and hides.

sentences.zh: 夾右碣石，入于海。

translations.literal: Flanking right Jieshi, entering the sea.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute went by way of Jieshi on the right and reached the sea.

translations.literal: Yu's progress began from Jizhou. Jizhou: already loaded at Hukou, regulated Liang and Qi. Already repaired Taiyuan, reaching the south of Mount Yue. Tanhuai achieved merit, reaching Heng and Zhang. Its soil was white loam. Revenue highest of highest with admixture; fields middle of middle; Chang and Wei already followed; the great plain was formed. Bird-barbarians offered furs. Flanking right Jieshi, entering the sea.
translations.idiomatic: Yu began his survey in Jizhou. In Jizhou he finished work at Hukou and brought order to the Liang and Qi ranges. He restored Taiyuan and continued south of Mount Yue. He completed the Tanhuai basin and carried the work to the Heng and Zhang rivers. The soil was pale, loose earth. Its tax was rated top grade with some second-grade tracts; its fields were middling; the Chang and Wei ran true, and the great alluvial plain emerged. Coastal peoples sent furs and hides. Tribute went by way of Jieshi on the right and reached the sea.

sentences.zh: 濟、河維沇州：九河既道，雷夏既澤，雍、沮會同，桑土既蠶，於是民得下丘居土。

translations.literal: Ji and He were Yanzhou: the nine rivers already had their courses; Leixia already was a marsh; Yong and Ju met together; mulberry soil already had silkworms; then the people could descend from heights and dwell on earth.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Ji and the Yellow River lay Yanzhou: the Nine Streams were channeled, Leixia became a lake, the Yong and Ju joined their waters, silkworms returned to the mulberry lands, and people could leave the heights for the plain.

sentences.zh: 其土黑墳，草繇木條。

translations.literal: Its soil was black and rich; grass luxuriant, trees straight.
translations.idiomatic: The ground was dark fertile loam with thick grass and tall straight timber.

sentences.zh: 田中下，賦貞，作十有三年乃同。

translations.literal: Fields lower middle; revenue fixed; after thirteen years then uniform.
translations.idiomatic: Fields ranked low-middle; the levy was set at the lowest regular grade until, after thirteen years of reclamation, it matched other districts.

sentences.zh: 其貢漆絲，其篚織文。

translations.literal: Its tribute lacquer and silk; its baskets patterned weave.
translations.idiomatic: It sent lacquer and silk in tribute, and woven figured cloth in baskets.

sentences.zh: 浮於濟、漯，通於河。

translations.literal: Floated on Ji and Ta, communicating with He.
translations.idiomatic: Shipments floated down the Ji and the Ta to join the Yellow River.

translations.literal: Ji and He were Yanzhou: the nine rivers already had their courses; Leixia already was a marsh; Yong and Ju met together; mulberry soil already had silkworms; then the people could descend from heights and dwell on earth. Its soil was black and rich; grass luxuriant, trees straight. Fields lower middle; revenue fixed; after thirteen years then uniform. Its tribute lacquer and silk; its baskets patterned weave. Floated on Ji and Ta, communicating with He.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Ji and the Yellow River lay Yanzhou: the Nine Streams were channeled, Leixia became a lake, the Yong and Ju joined their waters, silkworms returned to the mulberry lands, and people could leave the heights for the plain. The ground was dark fertile loam with thick grass and tall straight timber. Fields ranked low-middle; the levy was set at the lowest regular grade until, after thirteen years of reclamation, it matched other districts. It sent lacquer and silk in tribute, and woven figured cloth in baskets. Shipments floated down the Ji and the Ta to join the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 海岱維青州：堣夷既略，濰、淄其道。

translations.literal: Sea and Dai were Qingzhou: Yuyi already subdued; Wei and Zi had their courses.
translations.idiomatic: Between the sea and Mount Dai lay Qingzhou: the Yuyi tribes were pacified, and the Wei and Zi were channeled.

sentences.zh: 其土白墳，海濱廣潟，厥田斥鹵。

translations.literal: Its soil white loam; seashore broad marshes; its fields saline.
translations.idiomatic: The soil was pale loam along a coast of wide tidal flats, much of it salt-encrusted.

sentences.zh: 田上下，賦中上。

translations.literal: Fields upper lower; revenue middle upper.
translations.idiomatic: Fields ranked high among the lower grades; the tax was upper-middle.

sentences.zh: 厥貢鹽絺，海物維錯，岱畎絲、枲、鉛、松、怪石，萊夷為牧，其篚酓絲。

translations.literal: Its tribute salt and fine cloth; sea things various; Dai's valleys silk, hemp, lead, pine, strange stones; Laiyi herded; its baskets mulberry silk.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute included salt, fine linen, a miscellany of sea produce, silk and hemp from the Dai valleys, lead, pine, and odd stones; the Lai people sent pasture goods and silkworm baskets.

sentences.zh: 浮於汶，通於濟。

translations.literal: Floated on Wen, communicating with Ji.
translations.idiomatic: It went by the Wen and linked to the Ji.

translations.literal: Sea and Dai were Qingzhou: Yuyi already subdued; Wei and Zi had their courses. Its soil white loam; seashore broad marshes; its fields saline. Fields upper lower; revenue middle upper. Its tribute salt and fine cloth; sea things various; Dai's valleys silk, hemp, lead, pine, strange stones; Laiyi herded; its baskets mulberry silk. Floated on Wen, communicating with Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Between the sea and Mount Dai lay Qingzhou: the Yuyi tribes were pacified, and the Wei and Zi were channeled. The soil was pale loam along a coast of wide tidal flats, much of it salt-encrusted. Fields ranked high among the lower grades; the tax was upper-middle. Tribute included salt, fine linen, a miscellany of sea produce, silk and hemp from the Dai valleys, lead, pine, and odd stones; the Lai people sent pasture goods and silkworm baskets. It went by the Wen and linked to the Ji.

sentences.zh: 海岱及淮維徐州：淮、沂其治，蒙、羽其藝。

translations.literal: Sea, Dai, and Huai were Xuzhou: Huai and Yi were regulated; Meng and Yu were cultivated.
translations.idiomatic: Between sea, Dai, and Huai lay Xuzhou: the Huai and Yi were tamed, and the Meng and Yu ranges were opened to the plough.

sentences.zh: 大野既都，東原底平。

translations.literal: The great marsh already settled; the eastern plain reached level.
translations.idiomatic: Daye marsh was diked and the eastern lowlands lay smooth.

sentences.zh: 其土赤埴墳，草木漸包。

translations.literal: Its soil red sticky loam; grass and trees gradually encased.
translations.idiomatic: The earth was sticky red clay, slowly clothing itself again in grass and wood.

sentences.zh: 其田上中，賦中中。

translations.literal: Its fields upper middle; revenue middle middle.
translations.idiomatic: Fields ranked second grade; the tax was middling.

sentences.zh: 貢維土五色，羽畎夏狄，嶧陽孤桐，泗濱浮磬，淮夷蠙珠臮魚，其篚玄纖縞。

translations.literal: Tribute earth of five colors; summer pheasant plumes from Yu's valley; lone paulownia from Yiyang; floating chime-stones from the Si bank; pearls and fish from Huaiyi; its baskets black fine silk.
translations.idiomatic: It sent five-colored clays, pheasant tails from the Yu valley, lone paulownia from Yiyang, floating lithophones from the Si, pearls and fish from the Huai tribes, and baskets of fine dark silk.

sentences.zh: 浮于淮、泗，通于河。

translations.literal: Floated on Huai and Si, communicating with He.
translations.idiomatic: Shipments moved on the Huai and Si to reach the Yellow River.

translations.literal: Sea, Dai, and Huai were Xuzhou: Huai and Yi were regulated; Meng and Yu were cultivated. The great marsh already settled; the eastern plain reached level. Its soil red sticky loam; grass and trees gradually encased. Its fields upper middle; revenue middle middle. Tribute earth of five colors; summer pheasant plumes from Yu's valley; lone paulownia from Yiyang; floating chime-stones from the Si bank; pearls and fish from Huaiyi; its baskets black fine silk. Floated on Huai and Si, communicating with He.
translations.idiomatic: Between sea, Dai, and Huai lay Xuzhou: the Huai and Yi were tamed, and the Meng and Yu ranges were opened to the plough. Daye marsh was diked and the eastern lowlands lay smooth. The earth was sticky red clay, slowly clothing itself again in grass and wood. Fields ranked second grade; the tax was middling. It sent five-colored clays, pheasant tails from the Yu valley, lone paulownia from Yiyang, floating lithophones from the Si, pearls and fish from the Huai tribes, and baskets of fine dark silk. Shipments moved on the Huai and Si to reach the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 淮海維揚州：彭蠡既都，陽鳥所居。

translations.literal: Huai and sea were Yangzhou: Pengli already settled; where the yang birds dwell.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Huai and the sea lay Yangzhou: Lake Pengli was stabilized and became a haunt for wintering birds.

sentences.zh: 三江既入，震澤致定。

translations.literal: Three rivers already entered; Zhenze reached stability.
translations.idiomatic: The three great channels poured in, and Lake Tai was calmed.

sentences.zh: 竹箭既布。

translations.literal: Bamboo arrows already spread.
translations.idiomatic: Bamboo groves for arrows flourished far and wide.

sentences.zh: 其草惟夭，其木惟喬，其土涂泥。

translations.literal: Its grass only young; its trees only tall; its soil mud.
translations.idiomatic: Grasses were tender, trees towered, and the soil was mire.

sentences.zh: 田下下，賦下上上雜。

translations.literal: Fields lowest lowest; revenue lowest upper upper mixed.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were the poorest grade; the levy was the top of the bottom tier with some higher admixture.

sentences.zh: 貢金三品，瑤、琨、竹箭，齒、革、羽、旄，島夷卉服，其篚織貝，其包橘、柚錫貢。

translations.literal: Tribute metals three grades, jade, kun stone, bamboo arrows; teeth, hides, feathers, yak-hair; island barbarians grass-cloth; its baskets woven shells; its bundles oranges and pomelo granted tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute listed three grades of metal, jade, fine stone, bamboo arrows, ivory, hides, feathers, and yak-tail; island peoples sent plant-fiber dress; baskets held patterned shells; bundles of citrus were granted as occasional gifts.

sentences.zh: 均江海，通淮、泗。

translations.literal: Evened Jiang and sea, communicating Huai and Si.
translations.idiomatic: He linked the Yangtze to the sea and opened routes to the Huai and Si.

translations.literal: Huai and sea were Yangzhou: Pengli already settled; where the yang birds dwell. Three rivers already entered; Zhenze reached stability. Bamboo arrows already spread. Its grass only young; its trees only tall; its soil mud. Fields lowest lowest; revenue lowest upper upper mixed. Tribute metals three grades, jade, kun stone, bamboo arrows; teeth, hides, feathers, yak-hair; island barbarians grass-cloth; its baskets woven shells; its bundles oranges and pomelo granted tribute. Evened Jiang and sea, communicating Huai and Si.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Huai and the sea lay Yangzhou: Lake Pengli was stabilized and became a haunt for wintering birds. The three great channels poured in, and Lake Tai was calmed. Bamboo groves for arrows flourished far and wide. Grasses were tender, trees towered, and the soil was mire. Fields were the poorest grade; the levy was the top of the bottom tier with some higher admixture. Tribute listed three grades of metal, jade, fine stone, bamboo arrows, ivory, hides, feathers, and yak-tail; island peoples sent plant-fiber dress; baskets held patterned shells; bundles of citrus were granted as occasional gifts. He linked the Yangtze to the sea and opened routes to the Huai and Si.

sentences.zh: 荊及衡陽維荊州：江、漢朝宗于海。

translations.literal: Jing and south of Heng were Jingzhou: Jiang and Han paid court to the sea.
translations.idiomatic: From Mount Jing to south of Mount Heng lay Jingzhou: the Yangtze and Han flowed as tributaries to the sea.

sentences.zh: 九江甚中，沱、涔已道，云土、夢為治。

translations.literal: Nine Jiang very central; Tuo and Cen already channeled; cloud-earth and dream reached regulation.
translations.idiomatic: The nine channels of the middle Yangtze were cleared, the Tuo and Cen confined, and the Yunmeng marshes brought under control.

sentences.zh: 其土涂泥。

translations.literal: Its soil mud.
translations.idiomatic: The ground was alluvial muck.

sentences.zh: 田下中，賦上下。

translations.literal: Fields lower middle; revenue upper lower.
translations.idiomatic: Fields ranked low-middle; taxes upper-lower.

sentences.zh: 貢羽、旄、齒、革，金三品，杶、榦、栝、柏，礪、砥、砮、丹，維箘簬、楛，三國致貢其名，包匭菁茅，其篚玄纁璣組，九江入賜大龜。

translations.literal: Tribute feathers, yak-hair, teeth, hides, metals three grades, oak, catalpa, cypress, grindstones, whetstones, arrowheads, cinnabar, bamboo and wood of Jun and Lu, three states sent their named tribute, wrapped calamus and thatch, baskets black and red, pearl strings; nine Jiang entered great tortoise granted.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute ran to feathers, furs, ivory, hides, three grades of ore, hardwoods, whetstones, cinnabar, specialty bamboos—three chiefdoms each sent their specialties—wrapped iris and thatch, baskets of dark silk and strings of pearls, and from the nine streams the great tortoise as imperial gift.

sentences.zh: 浮于江、沱、涔、（于）漢，踰于雒，至于南河。

translations.literal: Floated on Jiang, Tuo, Cen, to Han, crossed to Luo, reaching south He.
translations.idiomatic: Goods floated on the Yangtze, Tuo, and Cen to the Han, then were portaged to the Luo and the southern Yellow River.

translations.literal: Jing and south of Heng were Jingzhou: Jiang and Han paid court to the sea. Nine Jiang very central; Tuo and Cen already channeled; cloud-earth and dream reached regulation. Its soil mud. Fields lower middle; revenue upper lower. Tribute feathers, yak-hair, teeth, hides, metals three grades, oak, catalpa, cypress, grindstones, whetstones, arrowheads, cinnabar, bamboo and wood of Jun and Lu, three states sent their named tribute, wrapped calamus and thatch, baskets black and red, pearl strings; nine Jiang entered great tortoise granted. Floated on Jiang, Tuo, Cen, to Han, crossed to Luo, reaching south He.
translations.idiomatic: From Mount Jing to south of Mount Heng lay Jingzhou: the Yangtze and Han flowed as tributaries to the sea. The nine channels of the middle Yangtze were cleared, the Tuo and Cen confined, and the Yunmeng marshes brought under control. The ground was alluvial muck. Fields ranked low-middle; taxes upper-lower. Tribute ran to feathers, furs, ivory, hides, three grades of ore, hardwoods, whetstones, cinnabar, specialty bamboos—three chiefdoms each sent their specialties—wrapped iris and thatch, baskets of dark silk and strings of pearls, and from the nine streams the great tortoise as imperial gift. Goods floated on the Yangtze, Tuo, and Cen to the Han, then were portaged to the Luo and the southern Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 荊河惟豫州：伊、雒、瀍、澗既入于河，滎播既都，道荷澤，被明都。

translations.literal: Jing and He were Yuzhou: Yi, Luo, Chan, and Jian already entered He; Xingbo already settled; channeled He marsh, spread Mingdu.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Jing mountains and the Yellow River lay Yuzhou: the Yi, Luo, Chan, and Jian were led into the main stream, Lake Xingbo was diked, the He marsh was opened, and Mingdu was flooded out.

sentences.zh: 其土壤，下土墳壚。

translations.literal: Its soil soil; lower soil loam.
translations.idiomatic: The upland was loess; the low ground heavier clay.

sentences.zh: 田中上，賦雜上中。

translations.literal: Fields middle upper; revenue mixed upper middle.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were second grade; the tax blended upper and middle rates.

sentences.zh: 貢漆、絲、絺、紵，其篚纖絮，錫貢磬錯。

translations.literal: Tribute lacquer, silk, fine cloth, hemp; its baskets fine floss; granted tribute chime-stone ore.
translations.idiomatic: It sent lacquer, silk, fine linen, and kudzu fiber in baskets of fine batting, with lithophone ore as an occasional gift.

sentences.zh: 浮於雒，達於河。

translations.literal: Floated on Luo, reaching He.
translations.idiomatic: Shipments floated down the Luo to the Yellow River.

translations.literal: Jing and He were Yuzhou: Yi, Luo, Chan, and Jian already entered He; Xingbo already settled; channeled He marsh, spread Mingdu. Its soil soil; lower soil loam. Fields middle upper; revenue mixed upper middle. Tribute lacquer, silk, fine cloth, hemp; its baskets fine floss; granted tribute chime-stone ore. Floated on Luo, reaching He.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Jing mountains and the Yellow River lay Yuzhou: the Yi, Luo, Chan, and Jian were led into the main stream, Lake Xingbo was diked, the He marsh was opened, and Mingdu was flooded out. The upland was loess; the low ground heavier clay. Fields were second grade; the tax blended upper and middle rates. It sent lacquer, silk, fine linen, and kudzu fiber in baskets of fine batting, with lithophone ore as an occasional gift. Shipments floated down the Luo to the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 華陽黑水惟梁州：汶、嶓既藝，沱、涔既道，蔡、蒙旅平，和夷厎績。

translations.literal: South of Hua and Black Water were Liangzhou: Min and Bo already cultivated; Tuo and Cen already channeled; Cai and Meng journeyed level; He yi reached merit.
translations.idiomatic: South of Mount Hua to the Black Water was Liangzhou: the Min and Bo were farmed, the Tuo and Cen confined, the Cai and Meng ranges cleared, and the He tribes brought in order.

sentences.zh: 其土青驪。

translations.literal: Its soil green-black.
translations.idiomatic: The soil was dark loam.

sentences.zh: 田下上，賦下中三錯。

translations.literal: Fields lower upper; revenue lower middle three mixed.
translations.idiomatic: Fields ranked low among the high grades; the levy shifted among three lower-middle levels.

sentences.zh: 貢璆、鐵、銀、鏤、砮、磬，熊、羆、狐、貍、織皮。

translations.literal: Tribute jade, iron, silver, carved bells, lithophones, bears, brown bears, foxes, wildcats, woven hides.
translations.idiomatic: It yielded fine jade, iron, silver, hammered metal, lithophones, and pelts of bear, brown bear, fox, and wildcat.

sentences.zh: 西傾因桓是來，浮于潛，踰于沔，入于渭，亂于河。

translations.literal: West Qin by Huan came; floated on Qian, crossed Mian, entered Wei, crossed He.
translations.idiomatic: From West Qin the route followed the Huan, then floated on the Qian, was portaged to the Mian, joined the Wei, and crossed to the Yellow River.

translations.literal: South of Hua and Black Water were Liangzhou: Min and Bo already cultivated; Tuo and Cen already channeled; Cai and Meng journeyed level; He yi reached merit. Its soil green-black. Fields lower upper; revenue lower middle three mixed. Tribute jade, iron, silver, carved bells, lithophones, bears, brown bears, foxes, wildcats, woven hides. West Qin by Huan came; floated on Qian, crossed Mian, entered Wei, crossed He.
translations.idiomatic: South of Mount Hua to the Black Water was Liangzhou: the Min and Bo were farmed, the Tuo and Cen confined, the Cai and Meng ranges cleared, and the He tribes brought in order. The soil was dark loam. Fields ranked low among the high grades; the levy shifted among three lower-middle levels. It yielded fine jade, iron, silver, hammered metal, lithophones, and pelts of bear, brown bear, fox, and wildcat. From West Qin the route followed the Huan, then floated on the Qian, was portaged to the Mian, joined the Wei, and crossed to the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 黑水西河惟雍州：弱水既西，涇屬渭汭。

translations.literal: Black Water west of He was Yongzhou: weak water already west; Jing joined Wei's north bank.
translations.idiomatic: West of the Black Water to the Yellow River was Yongzhou: the Weak River was turned westward, and the Jing joined the Wei at its bend.

sentences.zh: 漆、沮既從，灃水所同。

translations.literal: Qi and Ju already followed; Feng water shared the same.
translations.idiomatic: The Qi and Ju obeyed their channels, and the Feng mingled where appointed.

sentences.zh: 荊、岐已旅，終南、敦物至于鳥鼠。

translations.literal: Jing and Qi already journeyed; Zhongnan, Dunwu reached Niaoshu.
translations.idiomatic: The Jing and Qi ranges were cleared from Zhongnan and Dunwu to Niaoshu.

sentences.zh: 原隰厎績，至于都野。

translations.literal: High plain and low marsh reached merit, reaching Duye.
translations.idiomatic: High moors and sunken bogs were drained as far as Duye.

sentences.zh: 三危既度，三苗大序。

translations.literal: Three Perils already measured; Three Miao greatly ordered.
translations.idiomatic: The Sanwei region was surveyed, and the Sanmiao tribes were brought into line.

sentences.zh: 其土黃壤。

translations.literal: Its soil yellow loam.
translations.idiomatic: The earth was yellow loam.

sentences.zh: 田上上，賦中下。

translations.literal: Fields highest highest; revenue middle lower.
translations.idiomatic: Fields were the finest grade; the tax was set middle-lower to reflect distance.

sentences.zh: 貢璆、琳、瑯玕。

translations.literal: Tribute jade, fine jade, carnelian.
translations.idiomatic: It sent jade, fine stone, and carnelian.

sentences.zh: 浮于積石，至于龍門西河，會于渭汭。

translations.literal: Floated on Jishi, reaching Longmen west He, meeting at Wei's north bank.
translations.idiomatic: Shipments floated from Jishi to Longmen on the western Yellow River and gathered at the Wei's bend.

sentences.zh: 織皮昆侖、析支、渠搜，西戎即序。

translations.literal: Woven hides Kunlun, Xizhi, Qusou; western Rong already ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Tribute furs came from Kunlun, Xizhi, and Qusou, and the western Rong were enrolled.

translations.literal: Black Water west of He was Yongzhou: weak water already west; Jing joined Wei's north bank. Qi and Ju already followed; Feng water shared the same. Jing and Qi already journeyed; Zhongnan, Dunwu reached Niaoshu. High plain and low marsh reached merit, reaching Duye. Three Perils already measured; Three Miao greatly ordered. Its soil yellow loam. Fields highest highest; revenue middle lower. Tribute jade, fine jade, carnelian. Floated on Jishi, reaching Longmen west He, meeting at Wei's north bank. Woven hides Kunlun, Xizhi, Qusou; western Rong already ordered.
translations.idiomatic: West of the Black Water to the Yellow River was Yongzhou: the Weak River was turned westward, and the Jing joined the Wei at its bend. The Qi and Ju obeyed their channels, and the Feng mingled where appointed. The Jing and Qi ranges were cleared from Zhongnan and Dunwu to Niaoshu. High moors and sunken bogs were drained as far as Duye. The Sanwei region was surveyed, and the Sanmiao tribes were brought into line. The earth was yellow loam. Fields were the finest grade; the tax was set middle-lower to reflect distance. It sent jade, fine stone, and carnelian. Shipments floated from Jishi to Longmen on the western Yellow River and gathered at the Wei's bend. Tribute furs came from Kunlun, Xizhi, and Qusou, and the western Rong were enrolled.

sentences.zh: 道九山：汧及岐至于荊山，踰于河；

translations.literal: He opened nine mountains: Qian and Qi reaching Jingshan, crossing He.
translations.idiomatic: He traced nine mountain chains: from Qian and Qi to Jingshan, then across the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 壺口、雷首至于太嶽；

translations.literal: Hukou, Leishou reaching Taiyue.
translations.idiomatic: From Hukou and Leishou to Taiyue.

sentences.zh: 砥柱、析城至于王屋；

translations.literal: Dizhu, Xicheng reaching Wangwu.
translations.idiomatic: From Dizhu and Xicheng to Wangwu.

sentences.zh: 太行、常山至于碣石，入于海；

translations.literal: Taihang, Changshan reaching Jieshi, entering the sea.
translations.idiomatic: From Taihang and Hengshan to Jieshi and into the sea.

sentences.zh: 西傾、朱圉、鳥鼠至于太華；

translations.literal: West Qin, Zhuyu, Niaoshu reaching Taihua.
translations.idiomatic: From West Qin, Zhuyu, and Niaoshu to Taihua.

sentences.zh: 熊耳、外方、桐柏至于負尾；

translations.literal: Xiong'er, Waifang, Tongbai reaching Fuwei.
translations.idiomatic: From Xiong'er, Waifang, and Tongbai to Fuwei.

sentences.zh: 道嶓冢，至于荊山；

translations.literal: He opened Bozhong, reaching Jingshan.
translations.idiomatic: He opened the Bozhong range to Jingshan.

sentences.zh: 內方至于大別；

translations.literal: Neifang reaching Dabie.
translations.idiomatic: From Neifang to Dabie.

sentences.zh: 汶山之陽至衡山，過九江，至于敷淺原。

translations.literal: South of Mount Min's range to Hengshan, crossing nine Jiang, reaching Fushaoyuan.
translations.idiomatic: From the southern slopes of Minshan to Hengshan, across the nine streams, to Fushaoyuan.

translations.literal: He opened nine mountains: Qian and Qi reaching Jingshan, crossing He. Hukou, Leishou reaching Taiyue. Dizhu, Xicheng reaching Wangwu. Taihang, Changshan reaching Jieshi, entering the sea. West Qin, Zhuyu, Niaoshu reaching Taihua. Xiong'er, Waifang, Tongbai reaching Fuwei. He opened Bozhong, reaching Jingshan. Neifang reaching Dabie. South of Mount Min's range to Hengshan, crossing nine Jiang, reaching Fushaoyuan.
translations.idiomatic: He traced nine mountain chains: from Qian and Qi to Jingshan, then across the Yellow River. From Hukou and Leishou to Taiyue. From Dizhu and Xicheng to Wangwu. From Taihang and Hengshan to Jieshi and into the sea. From West Qin, Zhuyu, and Niaoshu to Taihua. From Xiong'er, Waifang, and Tongbai to Fuwei. He opened the Bozhong range to Jingshan. From Neifang to Dabie. From the southern slopes of Minshan to Hengshan, across the nine streams, to Fushaoyuan.

sentences.zh: 道九川：弱水至於合黎，餘波入于流沙。

translations.literal: He opened nine streams: weak water reaching Heli, surplus waves entering flowing sands.
translations.idiomatic: He channeled nine rivers: the Weak to Heli, its tail into the shifting sands.

sentences.zh: 道黑水，至于三危，入于南海。

translations.literal: He opened Black Water, reaching Three Perils, entering south sea.
translations.idiomatic: He led the Black Water to Sanwei and out into the southern sea.

sentences.zh: 道河積石，至于龍門，南至華陰，東至砥柱，又東至于盟津，東過雒汭，至于大邳，北過降水，至于大陸，北播為九河，同為逆河，入于海。

translations.literal: He opened He Jishi, reaching Longmen, south reaching Huayin, east reaching Dizhu, again east reaching Mengjin, east crossing Luo's bend, reaching Dapi, north crossing Jiangshui, reaching Dalu, north spreading as nine He, together reverse He, entering sea.
translations.idiomatic: He guided the Yellow River from Jishi and Longmen south to Huayin, east past the pillars at Mengjin, across the Luo bend to Dapi, north past the Jiang to the great alluvial plain, where it fanned into the nine channels and the backwash belt before reaching the sea.

sentences.zh: 嶓冢道瀁，東流為漢，又東為蒼浪之水，過三澨，入于大別，南入于江，東匯澤為彭蠡，東為北江，入于海。

translations.literal: Bozhong opened Yang, east flowing became Han, again east became Canglang water, passed three rapids, entered Dabie, south entered Jiang, east collected marsh as Pengli, east became north Jiang, entered sea.
translations.idiomatic: From Bozhong he opened the Han, turned it east through the Canglang reaches and three gorges to Dabie, then south into the Yangtze, east into Lake Pengli, and east again as the northern stream to the sea.

sentences.zh: 汶山道江，東別為沱，又東至于醴，過九江，至于東陵，東迆北會于匯，東為中江，入于梅。

translations.literal: Minshan opened Jiang, east divided became Tuo, again east reached Li, crossed nine Jiang, reached Dongling, east winding north met at Hui, east became middle Jiang, entered Mei.
translations.idiomatic: From Minshan he split the Yangtze's eastern arm as the Tuo, led it past the Li reaches and the nine channels to Dongling, bent north to the confluence, and opened the middle stream to the sea.

sentences.zh: 道沇水，東為濟，入于河，泆為滎，東出陶丘北，又東至于荷，又東北會于汶，又東北入于海。

translations.literal: He opened Yan water, east became Ji, entered He, overflowed became Xing, east emerged north of Taoqiu, again east reached He, again northeast met Wen, again northeast entered sea.
translations.idiomatic: He channeled the Yan into the Ji to the Yellow River, where it burst forth as the Xing pool, ran east from north of Taoqiu to the He marsh, then northeast by the Wen to the sea.

sentences.zh: 道淮自桐柏，東會于泗、沂，東入于海。

translations.literal: He opened Huai from Tongbai, east met Si and Yi, east entered sea.
translations.idiomatic: He traced the Huai from Tongbai east to join the Si and Yi, then to the sea.

sentences.zh: 道渭自鳥鼠同穴，東會于灃，又東北至于涇，東過漆、沮，入于河。

translations.literal: He opened Wei from Niaoshu same-burrow, east met Feng, again northeast reached Jing, east crossing Qi and Ju, entered He.
translations.idiomatic: He opened the Wei from Niaoshu, east to the Feng, northeast to the Jing, then past the Qi and Ju into the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 道雒自熊耳，東北會于澗、瀍，又東會于伊，東北入于河。

translations.literal: He opened Luo from Xiong'er, northeast met Jian and Chan, again east met Yi, northeast entered He.
translations.idiomatic: He led the Luo from Xiong'er northeast through the Jian and Chan, east to meet the Yi, and northeast into the Yellow River.

translations.literal: He opened nine streams: weak water reaching Heli, surplus waves entering flowing sands. He opened Black Water, reaching Three Perils, entering south sea. He opened He Jishi, reaching Longmen, south reaching Huayin, east reaching Dizhu, again east reaching Mengjin, east crossing Luo's bend, reaching Dapi, north crossing Jiangshui, reaching Dalu, north spreading as nine He, together reverse He, entering sea. Bozhong opened Yang, east flowing became Han, again east became Canglang water, passed three rapids, entered Dabie, south entered Jiang, east collected marsh as Pengli, east became north Jiang, entered sea. Minshan opened Jiang, east divided became Tuo, again east reached Li, crossed nine Jiang, reached Dongling, east winding north met at Hui, east became middle Jiang, entered Mei. He opened Yan water, east became Ji, entered He, overflowed became Xing, east emerged north of Taoqiu, again east reached He, again northeast met Wen, again northeast entered sea. He opened Huai from Tongbai, east met Si and Yi, east entered sea. He opened Wei from Niaoshu same-burrow, east met Feng, again northeast reached Jing, east crossing Qi and Ju, entered He. He opened Luo from Xiong'er, northeast met Jian and Chan, again east met Yi, northeast entered He.
translations.idiomatic: He channeled nine rivers: the Weak to Heli, its tail into the shifting sands. He led the Black Water to Sanwei and out into the southern sea. He guided the Yellow River from Jishi and Longmen south to Huayin, east past the pillars at Mengjin, across the Luo bend to Dapi, north past the Jiang to the great alluvial plain, where it fanned into the nine channels and the backwash belt before reaching the sea. From Bozhong he opened the Han, turned it east through the Canglang reaches and three gorges to Dabie, then south into the Yangtze, east into Lake Pengli, and east again as the northern stream to the sea. From Minshan he split the Yangtze's eastern arm as the Tuo, led it past the Li reaches and the nine channels to Dongling, bent north to the confluence, and opened the middle stream to the sea. He channeled the Yan into the Ji to the Yellow River, where it burst forth as the Xing pool, ran east from north of Taoqiu to the He marsh, then northeast by the Wen to the sea. He traced the Huai from Tongbai east to join the Si and Yi, then to the sea. He opened the Wei from Niaoshu, east to the Feng, northeast to the Jing, then past the Qi and Ju into the Yellow River. He led the Luo from Xiong'er northeast through the Jian and Chan, east to meet the Yi, and northeast into the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 於是九州攸同，四奧既居，九山刊旅，九川滌原，九澤既陂，四海會同。

translations.literal: Thereupon nine provinces together; four depths already dwelt; nine mountains cut and journeyed; nine streams washed source; nine marshes already banked; four seas met together.
translations.idiomatic: Then the nine provinces were one domain; the four gulfs had homes; ranges were cleared; rivers scoured their beds; marshes were diked; and the four seas became one waterway.

sentences.zh: 六府甚修，眾土交正，致慎財賦，咸則三壤成賦。

translations.literal: Six treasuries very cultivated; all soils mutually corrected; careful with wealth and levies; all modeled three soils completing revenue.
translations.idiomatic: The six storehouses were stocked; soils were assayed; taxes were set with care; every grade of earth paid its due.

sentences.zh: 中國賜土姓：「祗臺德先，不距朕行。」

translations.literal: Central state granted earth surnames: "Revere and put virtue first; do not resist my course."
translations.idiomatic: The central lands received clan names and were told: "Honor virtue before all and do not defy my rule."

translations.literal: Thereupon nine provinces together; four depths already dwelt; nine mountains cut and journeyed; nine streams washed source; nine marshes already banked; four seas met together. Six treasuries very cultivated; all soils mutually corrected; careful with wealth and levies; all modeled three soils completing revenue. Central state granted earth surnames: "Revere and put virtue first; do not resist my course."
translations.idiomatic: Then the nine provinces were one domain; the four gulfs had homes; ranges were cleared; rivers scoured their beds; marshes were diked; and the four seas became one waterway. The six storehouses were stocked; soils were assayed; taxes were set with care; every grade of earth paid its due. The central lands received clan names and were told: "Honor virtue before all and do not defy my rule."

sentences.zh: 令天子之國以外五百里甸服：百里賦納總，二百里納銍，三百里納秸服，四百里粟，五百里米。

translations.literal: He ordered beyond the Son of Heaven's domain five hundred li field-domain: hundred li tribute bound sheaves, two hundred li ears of grain, three hundred li straw service, four hundred li grain, five hundred li polished rice.
translations.idiomatic: He fixed the five-hundred-li royal domain: the inner hundred li sent whole stalks, the next grain ears, the next fodder and labor, then millet, then rice at the rim.

sentences.zh: 甸服外五百里侯服：百里采，二百里任國，三百里諸侯。

translations.literal: Field-domain outer five hundred li lord-domain: hundred li gathering, two hundred li duty states, three hundred li feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Beyond that lay another five hundred li for the lords: inner districts for court estates, next for assigned service, outer for the feudal order.

sentences.zh: 侯服外五百里綏服：三百里揆文教，二百里奮武衛。

translations.literal: Lord-domain outer five hundred li peace-domain: three hundred li measure culture-teaching, two hundred li vigorous martial guard.
translations.idiomatic: Outside that came the pacification belt: inner tracts for civil instruction, outer for military screen.

sentences.zh: 綏服外五百里要服：三百里夷，二百里蔡。

translations.literal: Peace-domain outer five hundred li binding-domain: three hundred li yi, two hundred li cai.
translations.idiomatic: Next was the coercive belt: inner Yi peoples, outer Cai settlements.

sentences.zh: 要服外五百里荒服：三百里蠻，二百里流。

translations.literal: Binding-domain outer five hundred li wild-domain: three hundred li man, two hundred li flow.
translations.idiomatic: Outermost lay the wilds: inner Man tribes, outer exiles.

Outside that came the pacification belt: inner tracts for civil instruction, outer for military screen. Next was the coercive belt: inner Yi peoples, outer Cai settlements. Outermost lay the wilds: inner Man tribes, outer exiles.

sentences.zh: 東漸于海，西被于流沙，朔、南暨：聲教訖于四海。

translations.literal: East washed to sea, west covered to shifting sands, north and south reached: sound and teaching ended at four seas.
translations.idiomatic: East to the ocean, west to the sands, north and south alike—his influence reached every shore.

sentences.zh: 於是帝錫禹玄圭，以告成功于天下。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Emperor granted Yu dark jade tablet, thereby announcing success to all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor gave Yu a black scepter to proclaim the work finished to the realm.

sentences.zh: 天下於是太平治。

translations.literal: All under Heaven thereupon greatly peaceful order.
translations.idiomatic: The realm then knew great peace.

translations.literal: East washed to sea, west covered to shifting sands, north and south reached: sound and teaching ended at four seas. Thereupon the Emperor granted Yu dark jade tablet, thereby announcing success to all under Heaven. All under Heaven thereupon greatly peaceful order.
translations.idiomatic: East to the ocean, west to the sands, north and south alike—his influence reached every shore. The Emperor gave Yu a black scepter to proclaim the work finished to the realm. The realm then knew great peace.

sentences.zh: 皋陶作士以理民。

translations.literal: Gao Yao served as minister to regulate the people.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao acted as judge to keep order among the people.

sentences.zh: 帝舜朝，禹、伯夷、皋陶相與語帝前。

translations.literal: Emperor Shun at court, Yu, Boyi, Gao Yao conversed with each other before the Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: At Shun's audience Yu, Boyi, and Gao Yao debated policy before the throne.

sentences.zh: 皋陶述其謀曰：「信其道德，謀明輔和。」

translations.literal: Gao Yao set forth his plan, saying, "Trust his virtue and way; counsel clear, assist harmony."
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao said, "Hold fast to true virtue and clear counsel, and harmony will follow."

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「然，如何？」

translations.literal: Yu said, "Yes—how shall we do it?"
translations.idiomatic: Yu asked, "Agreed—but how do we put that into practice?"

sentences.zh: 皋陶曰：「於！

translations.literal: Gao Yao said, "Oh!"
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao cried, "Ah!"

sentences.zh: 慎其身修，思長，敦序九族，眾明高翼，近可遠在已。」

translations.literal: Careful with body cultivation, thinking long, generous ordering nine kin, many bright high wings, near can far in self.
translations.idiomatic: Watch your own conduct, plan far ahead, cherish the nine branches of kin, lift up the worthy, and what is near in yourself will reach what is far.

sentences.zh: 禹拜美言，曰：「然。」

translations.literal: Yu bowed praising the words, saying, "Yes."
translations.idiomatic: Yu bowed and said, "Well spoken—it is so."

sentences.zh: 皋陶曰：「於！

translations.literal: Gao Yao said, "Oh!"
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao continued, "Ah!"

sentences.zh: 在知人，在安民。」

translations.literal: In knowing men, in pacifying the people.
translations.idiomatic: Know your officers and secure the people.

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「吁！

translations.literal: Yu said, "Alas!"
translations.idiomatic: Yu cried, "Oh—"

sentences.zh: 皆若是，惟帝其難之。

translations.literal: If all were thus, only the Emperor would find it hard.
translations.idiomatic: If everyone did that, even a sage on the throne would find it hard.

sentences.zh: 知人則智，能官人；

translations.literal: Knowing men then wise, able to appoint men;
translations.idiomatic: To know men is wisdom and lets you place each in his post.

sentences.zh: 能安民則惠，黎民懷之。

translations.literal: able to pacify people then kind, black-haired people cherish him.
translations.idiomatic: To pacify the people is kindness, and the common folk will cleave to you.

sentences.zh: 能知能惠，何憂乎驩兜，何遷乎有苗，何畏乎巧言善色佞人？」

translations.literal: Able to know able to kind, what worry about Huandou, what banish about Youmiao, what fear about clever words fine faces flatterers?
translations.idiomatic: With both wisdom and kindness, why fear Huandou, why exile the Miao, why dread smooth talk and pretty faces?

sentences.zh: 皋陶曰：「然，於！

translations.literal: Gao Yao said, "Yes—oh!"
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao said, "Just so—indeed!"

sentences.zh: 亦行有九德，亦言其有德。」

translations.literal: Also conduct has nine virtues; also speech has its virtue.
translations.idiomatic: In conduct there are nine virtues; in words there are signs of them.

sentences.zh: 乃言曰：「始事事，寬而栗，柔而立，願而共，治而敬，擾而毅，直而溫，簡而廉，剛而實，彊而義，章其有常，吉哉。

translations.literal: Then saying, "Begin affairs: broad yet firm, soft yet standing, obedient yet respectful, ordered yet reverent, adaptable yet resolute, straight yet warm, simple yet incorrupt, hard yet solid, strong yet righteous—manifest their constancy—auspicious."
translations.idiomatic: They are: to be generous yet disciplined, gentle yet firm, tractable yet dignified, orderly yet reverent, teachable yet resolute, candid yet mild, spare yet honest, stern yet true, forceful yet just. Show these as steady habits, and all will be well.

sentences.zh: 日宣三德，蚤夜翊明有家。

translations.literal: Daily spread three virtues, early night assist bright having family.
translations.idiomatic: Practice three virtues each day, morning and night, and you may govern a household.

sentences.zh: 日嚴振敬六德，亮采有國。

translations.literal: Daily strict shake reverence six virtues, bright gathering having state.
translations.idiomatic: Strictly maintain six virtues with daily care, and you may rule a state.

sentences.zh: 翕受普施，九德咸事，俊乂在官，百吏肅謹。

translations.literal: Receive together spread broadly, nine virtues all affairs, outstanding ones in office, hundred clerks reverent careful.
translations.idiomatic: Welcome all talents, put the nine virtues to work, place the able in office, and every clerk will stand in awe.

sentences.zh: 毋教邪淫奇謀。

translations.literal: Do not teach deviant evil strange plots.
translations.idiomatic: Never teach lewd ways or crooked schemes.

sentences.zh: 非其人居其官，是謂亂天事。

translations.literal: Not its man dwelling its office—this is called disorder Heaven's affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Put the wrong man in a post and you throw Heaven's business into chaos.

sentences.zh: 天討有罪，五刑五用哉。

translations.literal: Heaven punishes guilty; five punishments five applications.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven punishes crime; the five penalties have their five uses.

sentences.zh: 吾言厎可行乎？」

translations.literal: My words bottom can be practiced?
translations.idiomatic: Can my words be carried out in practice?

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「女言致可績行。」

translations.literal: Yu said, "Your words reach can be traced in practice."
translations.idiomatic: Yu said, "What you say can be carried out and will show results."

sentences.zh: 皋陶曰：「余未有知，思贊道哉。」

translations.literal: Gao Yao said, "I have no knowledge; I think only to assist the way."
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao said, "I know little myself—I only wish to help along the right path."

sentences.zh: 帝舜謂禹曰：「女亦昌言。」

translations.literal: Emperor Shun told Yu, "You also speak expansively."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun said to Yu, "You speak plainly in your turn."

sentences.zh: 禹拜曰；

translations.literal: Yu bowed saying;
translations.idiomatic: Yu bowed to speak.

sentences.zh: 「於，予何言！

translations.literal: Oh, what shall I say!
translations.idiomatic: What more is there to say!

sentences.zh: 予思日孳孳。」

translations.literal: I think daily diligent diligent.
translations.idiomatic: Day and night I have only labored without rest.

sentences.zh: 皋陶難禹曰：「何謂孳孳？」

translations.literal: Gao Yao pressed Yu saying, "What is called diligent diligent?"
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao pressed him: "What do you mean by striving without ceasing?"

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「鴻水滔天，浩浩懷山襄陵，下民皆服於水。

translations.literal: Yu said, Vast flood swelled heaven, vastly embraced mountains overflowed hills, lower people all submerged in water.
translations.idiomatic: Yu answered: When the flood rose to the sky, wrapped the hills and drowned the valleys, the people were trapped in the waters.

sentences.zh: 予陸行乘車，水行乘舟，泥行乘橇，山行乘檋，行山刊木。

translations.literal: I land rode carriage, water rode boat, mud rode sledge, mountain rode litter, marched mountains cut trees.
translations.idiomatic: I rode a carriage on dry ground, boats on the water, sledges in mud, litters in the hills, and cut paths through the forests.

sentences.zh: 與益予眾庶稻鮮食。

translations.literal: Together with Yi gave multitude rice fresh food.
translations.idiomatic: With Yi I gave the people grain and fresh meat from the marshes.

sentences.zh: 以決九川致四海，浚畎澮致之川。

translations.literal: To decide nine streams reach four seas, dredged ditches channels reach them streams.
translations.idiomatic: I opened the nine rivers to the four seas and deepened the field channels into the trunk streams.

sentences.zh: 與稷予眾庶難得之食。

translations.literal: Together with Ji gave multitude hard-to-obtain food.
translations.idiomatic: With Ji I supplied food wherever famine struck.

sentences.zh: 食少，調有餘補不足，徙居。

translations.literal: Food scarce, adjusted surplus mending shortage, moved dwellings.
translations.idiomatic: Where grain ran short I shifted surplus to cover want and resettled the people.

sentences.zh: 眾民乃定，萬國為治。」

translations.literal: Multitude people then settled, myriad states for order.
translations.idiomatic: Then the masses found a home and the myriad regions were governed.

sentences.zh: 皋陶曰：「然，此而美也。」

translations.literal: Gao Yao said, "Yes—this indeed beautiful."
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao said, "Truly—this is the finest of policies."

translations.literal: Emperor Shun told Yu, "You also speak expansively." Yu bowed saying; Oh, what shall I say! I think daily diligent diligent. Gao Yao pressed Yu saying, "What is called diligent diligent?" Yu said, Vast flood swelled heaven, vastly embraced mountains overflowed hills, lower people all submerged in water. I land rode carriage, water rode boat, mud rode sledge, mountain rode litter, marched mountains cut trees. Together with Yi gave multitude rice fresh food. To decide nine streams reach four seas, dredged ditches channels reach them streams. Together with Ji gave multitude hard-to-obtain food. Food scarce, adjusted surplus mending shortage, moved dwellings. Multitude people then settled, myriad states for order. Gao Yao said, "Yes—this indeed beautiful."

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「於，帝！

translations.literal: Yu said, Oh, Emperor!
translations.idiomatic: Yu said: My lord—

sentences.zh: 慎乃在位，安爾止。

translations.literal: Careful with your occupying the throne, secure your resting.
translations.idiomatic: Watch how you sit the throne and keep your footing steady.

sentences.zh: 輔德，天下大應。

translations.literal: Assist virtue, all under Heaven greatly respond.
translations.idiomatic: Support true virtue, and the whole realm will answer you.

sentences.zh: 清意以昭待上帝命，天其重命用休。」

translations.literal: Clear mind thereby bright await Supreme God's commands; Heaven will again mandate use blessing.
translations.idiomatic: With a clear heart wait on Heaven's command, and Heaven will renew its blessing.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「吁，臣哉，臣哉！

translations.literal: Emperor said, Alas, ministers! ministers!
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor said: O ministers, ministers!

sentences.zh: 臣作朕股肱耳目。

translations.literal: Ministers are my thighs arms eyes ears.
translations.idiomatic: You are my legs, arms, eyes, and ears.

sentences.zh: 予欲左右有民，女輔之。

translations.literal: I desire assist have people, you assist them.
translations.idiomatic: I wish to help the people—aid me in it.

sentences.zh: 余欲觀古人之象。

translations.literal: I desire observe ancient men's images.
translations.idiomatic: I wish to read the pattern of the ancients.

sentences.zh: 日月星辰，作文繡服色，女明之。

translations.literal: Sun moon stars, make patterns embroidered colors, you clarify them.
translations.idiomatic: Sun, moon, and stars should pattern robes and insignia—you shall set them clear.

sentences.zh: 予欲聞六律五聲八音，來始滑，以出入五言，女聽。

translations.literal: I desire hear six pipes five tones eight sounds, come begin smooth, to go out enter five words, you listen.
translations.idiomatic: I would hear the six pitch-pipes, five notes, and eight timbres in harmony—you shall attune them.

sentences.zh: 予即辟，女匡拂予。

translations.literal: I if biased, you correct assist me.
translations.idiomatic: If I go astray, correct and steady me.

sentences.zh: 女無面諛。

translations.literal: You do not face flatter.
translations.idiomatic: Do not flatter me to my face.

sentences.zh: 退而謗予。

translations.literal: Retiring then slander me.
translations.idiomatic: Nor slander me behind my back.

sentences.zh: 敬四輔臣。

translations.literal: Revere the four supporting ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Honor the four pillar ministers.

sentences.zh: 諸眾讒嬖臣，君德誠施皆清矣。」

translations.literal: As for many slander favorite ministers, lord virtue sincerely spread all clear.
translations.idiomatic: If slanderers and favorites abound, let your true virtue shine through and all will be clear.

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「然。

translations.literal: Yu said, Yes.
translations.idiomatic: Yu said, Yes.

sentences.zh: 帝即不時，布同善惡則毋功。」

translations.literal: Emperor if not timely, spread same good evil then no merit.
translations.idiomatic: If the Son of Heaven is out of step and spreads the same measure to good and evil alike, we shall accomplish nothing.

I desire assist have people, you assist them. I desire observe ancient men's images. Sun moon stars, make patterns embroidered colors, you clarify them. I desire hear six pipes five tones eight sounds, come begin smooth, to go out enter five words, you listen. I if biased, you correct assist me. You do not face flatter. Retiring then slander me. If the Son of Heaven is out of step and spreads the same measure to good and evil alike, we shall accomplish nothing.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「毋若丹朱傲，維慢游是好，毋水行舟，朋淫于家，用絕其世。

translations.literal: Emperor said, Do not be like Danzhu arrogant, only slow wandering is his love, only water go boat, flock licentious in house, use cut off his generations.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor said: Do not be like Danzhu, proud and idle, who boated for pleasure, debauched his household, and lost his line.

sentences.zh: 予不能順是。」

translations.literal: I cannot follow this.
translations.idiomatic: I cannot follow such a course.

sentences.zh: 禹曰：「予（辛壬）娶涂山，［辛壬］癸甲，生啟予不子，以故能成水土功。

translations.literal: Yu said, "I marry Tushan on xinren, guijia, bore Qi I not treat as son, thereby able complete earth-water merit.
translations.idiomatic: Yu said, "I wed a woman of Tushan after four days away; when our son Qi was born I did not dote on him—thus I finished the flood work."

sentences.zh: 輔成五服，至于五千里，州十二師，外薄四海，咸建五長，各道有功。

translations.literal: Assist complete five domains, reaching five thousand li, twelve divisions per province, outer thin four seas, all establish five chiefs, each guide having merit.
translations.idiomatic: I helped shape the five zones out to five thousand li, set twelve divisions in each province, reached to the rim of the seas, appointed five regional overseers, and urged every district to earn its keep.

sentences.zh: 苗頑不即功，帝其念哉。」

translations.literal: Miao stubborn not approach merit, Emperor should think on them.
translations.idiomatic: Only the Miao remained stubborn—think of them, my lord.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「道吾德，乃女功序之也。」

translations.literal: Emperor said, "Conducting my virtue is your ordering the merit."
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor answered, "It is your ordering of merit that spreads my virtue."
