sentences.zh: 皋陶於是敬禹之德，令民皆則禹。

translations.literal: Gao Yao thereupon revered Yu's virtue, ordered people all model Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao then honored Yu's example and told the people to imitate him.

sentences.zh: 不如言，刑從之。

translations.literal: If not matching words, punishment follows them.
translations.idiomatic: Those who defied his commands faced the law.

sentences.zh: 舜德大明。

translations.literal: Shun's virtue greatly bright.
translations.idiomatic: Shun's virtue shone the brighter for it.

translations.literal: Gao Yao thereupon revered Yu's virtue, ordered people all model Yu. If not matching words, punishment follows them. Shun's virtue greatly bright.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao then honored Yu's example and told the people to imitate him. Those who defied his commands faced the law. Shun's virtue shone the brighter for it.

sentences.zh: 於是夔行樂，祖考至，群后相讓，鳥獸翔舞，簫韶九成，鳳皇來儀，百獸率舞，百官信諧。

translations.literal: Thereupon Kui performed music, ancestors arrived, host of lords yielding to each other, birds beasts soaring dance, xiaoshao nine completions, phoenixes came pattern, hundred beasts lead dance, hundred officials truly harmonious.
translations.idiomatic: Then Kui played the Nine Shao; the shades of the ancestors drew near; the lords yielded to one another; birds and beasts danced; phoenixes appeared; every creature joined the measure, and the officers moved in perfect accord.

sentences.zh: 帝用此作歌曰：「陟天之命，維時維幾。」

translations.literal: Emperor using this made song, saying, "Ascend Heaven's mandate, only season only danger."
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor made a song: "Heaven's charge is high—mind the seasons and the perilous moments."

sentences.zh: 乃歌曰：「股肱喜哉，元首起哉，百工熙哉！」

translations.literal: Then sang, "Thighs arms joyful! Head rises! Hundred crafts bright!"
translations.idiomatic: He sang, "Joy to our limbs! Awake, our head! Brightly thrive all crafts!"

sentences.zh: 皋陶拜手稽首揚言曰：「念哉，率為興事，慎乃憲，敬哉！」

translations.literal: Gao Yao bowing hand touching head raised saying, "Think! Lead to raise affairs, careful your model, reverent!"
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao bowed and cried, "Remember this! Act to raise new works; watch your pattern; be reverent!"

sentences.zh: 乃更為歌曰：「元首明哉，股肱良哉，庶事康哉！」

translations.literal: Then changed making song, saying, "Head bright! Thighs arms good! Multitude matters peaceful!"
translations.idiomatic: He sang in turn, "The head is wise! The limbs are good! All affairs are at peace!"

sentences.zh: （舜）又歌曰：「元首叢脞哉，股肱惰哉，萬事墮哉！」

translations.literal: (Shun) again sang, "Head cluttered! Thighs arm lazy! Myriad matters ruin!"
translations.idiomatic: Shun sang again, "If the head is muddled, the limbs grow slack, and every task collapses!"

sentences.zh: 帝拜曰：「然，往欽哉！」

translations.literal: Emperor bowing said, "Yes, go reverent!"
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor bowed and said, "Yes—go and be reverent!"

sentences.zh: 於是天下皆宗禹之明度數聲樂，為山川神主。

translations.literal: Thereupon all under Heaven all took as model Yu's bright measures numbers sound music, for mountains rivers spirit lord.
translations.idiomatic: All the realm then took Yu's calendrics, measures, and music as their model and made him lord of hills and streams.

translations.literal: Thereupon Kui performed music, ancestors arrived, host of lords yielding to each other, birds beasts soaring dance, xiaoshao nine completions, phoenixes came pattern, hundred beasts lead dance, hundred officials truly harmonious. Emperor using this made song, saying, "Ascend Heaven's mandate, only season only danger." Then sang, "Thighs arms joyful! Head rises! Hundred crafts bright!" Gao Yao bowing hand touching head raised saying, "Think! Lead to raise affairs, careful your model, reverent!" Then changed making song, saying, "Head bright! Thighs arms good! Multitude matters peaceful!" (Shun) again sang, "Head cluttered! Thighs arm lazy! Myriad matters ruin!" Emperor bowing said, "Yes, go reverent!" Thereupon all under Heaven all took as model Yu's bright measures numbers sound music, for mountains rivers spirit lord.
translations.idiomatic: Then Kui played the Nine Shao; the shades of the ancestors drew near; the lords yielded to one another; birds and beasts danced; phoenixes appeared; every creature joined the measure, and the officers moved in perfect accord. The Emperor made a song: "Heaven's charge is high—mind the seasons and the perilous moments." He sang, "Joy to our limbs! Awake, our head! Brightly thrive all crafts!" Gao Yao bowed and cried, "Remember this! Act to raise new works; watch your pattern; be reverent!" He sang in turn, "The head is wise! The limbs are good! All affairs are at peace!" Shun sang again, "If the head is muddled, the limbs grow slack, and every task collapses!" The Emperor bowed and said, "Yes—go and be reverent!" All the realm then took Yu's calendrics, measures, and music as their model and made him lord of hills and streams.

sentences.zh: 帝舜薦禹於天，為嗣。

translations.literal: Emperor Shun recommended Yu to Heaven, as heir.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun presented Yu to Heaven as successor.

sentences.zh: 十七年而帝舜崩。

translations.literal: Seventeen years then Emperor Shun passed away.
translations.idiomatic: Seventeen years later Emperor Shun died.

sentences.zh: 三年喪畢，禹辭辟舜之子商均於陽城。

translations.literal: Three years mourning finished, Yu declined yielding to Shun's son Shangjun at Yangcheng.
translations.idiomatic: When the three years' mourning ended, Yu withdrew to Yangcheng to yield to Shun's son Shangjun.

sentences.zh: 天下諸侯皆去商均而朝禹。

translations.literal: All under Heaven lords all left Shangjun and courted Yu.
translations.idiomatic: The lords of the realm forsook Shangjun and turned to Yu.

sentences.zh: 禹於是遂即天子位，南面朝天下，國號曰夏后，姓姒氏。

translations.literal: Yu thereupon then assumed Son of Heaven position, south faced all under Heaven, state title said Xia hou, surname Si clan.
translations.idiomatic: Yu then took the throne, faced south over the realm, styled his dynasty Xia, and took the clan name Si.

translations.literal: Emperor Shun recommended Yu to Heaven, as heir. Seventeen years then Emperor Shun passed away. Three years mourning finished, Yu declined yielding to Shun's son Shangjun at Yangcheng. All under Heaven lords all left Shangjun and courted Yu. Yu thereupon then assumed Son of Heaven position, south faced all under Heaven, state title said Xia hou, surname Si clan.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun presented Yu to Heaven as successor. Seventeen years later Emperor Shun died. When the three years' mourning ended, Yu withdrew to Yangcheng to yield to Shun's son Shangjun. The lords of the realm forsook Shangjun and turned to Yu. Yu then took the throne, faced south over the realm, styled his dynasty Xia, and took the clan name Si.

sentences.zh: 帝禹立而舉皋陶薦之，且授政焉，而皋陶卒。

translations.literal: Emperor Yu established and raised Gao Yao recommending him, and was about to transfer government, but Gao Yao died.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Yu came to power he promoted Gao Yao and meant to hand him the government, but Gao Yao died.

sentences.zh: 封皋陶之後於英、六，或在許。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed Gao Yao's posterity at Ying and Liu, or at Xu.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed Gao Yao's descendants at Ying, Liu, and perhaps Xu.

sentences.zh: 而后舉益，任之政。

translations.literal: Then afterward raised Yi, entrusted him government.
translations.idiomatic: Later he raised Yi and entrusted him with administration.

translations.literal: Emperor Yu established and raised Gao Yao recommending him, and was about to transfer government, but Gao Yao died. He enfeoffed Gao Yao's posterity at Ying and Liu, or at Xu. Then afterward raised Yi, entrusted him government.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Yu came to power he promoted Gao Yao and meant to hand him the government, but Gao Yao died. He enfeoffed Gao Yao's descendants at Ying, Liu, and perhaps Xu. Later he raised Yi and entrusted him with administration.

sentences.zh: 十年，帝禹東巡狩，至于會稽而崩。

translations.literal: Ten years, Emperor Yu east toured inspection, reaching Kuaiji then passed away.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year of his reign Yu toured east to Kuaiji and died there.

sentences.zh: 以天下授益。

translations.literal: He gave all under Heaven to Yi.
translations.idiomatic: He left the realm to Yi.

sentences.zh: 三年之喪畢，益讓帝禹之子啟，而辟居箕山之陽。

translations.literal: Three years mourning finished, Yi yielded to Emperor Yu's son Qi, and declined dwelling south of Mount Ji.
translations.idiomatic: After the mourning Yi yielded to Yu's son Qi and withdrew south of Mount Ji.

sentences.zh: 禹子啟賢，天下屬意焉。

translations.literal: Yu's son Qi worthy, all under Heaven fixed intent on him.
translations.idiomatic: Yu's son Qi was worthy, and the realm's hopes settled on him.

sentences.zh: 及禹崩，雖授益，益之佐禹日淺，天下未洽。

translations.literal: When Yu passed away, although he gave Yi, Yi's assisting Yu days shallow, all under Heaven not harmonious.
translations.idiomatic: Though Yu had named Yi, Yi had served at Yu's side too briefly to win the people's trust.

sentences.zh: 故諸侯皆去益而朝啟，曰：「吾君帝禹之子也」。

translations.literal: Therefore lords all left Yi and courted Qi, saying, "Our lord is Emperor Yu's son."
translations.idiomatic: So the lords left Yi for Qi, saying, "He is our lord, the Son of Yu."

sentences.zh: 於是啟遂即天子之位，是為夏后帝啟。

translations.literal: Thereupon Qi then assumed Son of Heaven position, this is Xia hou Emperor Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Qi then mounted the throne as Emperor Qi of Xia.

translations.literal: Ten years, Emperor Yu east toured inspection, reaching Kuaiji then passed away. He gave all under Heaven to Yi. Three years mourning finished, Yi yielded to Emperor Yu's son Qi, and declined dwelling south of Mount Ji. Yu's son Qi worthy, all under Heaven fixed intent on him. When Yu passed away, although he gave Yi, Yi's assisting Yu days shallow, all under Heaven not harmonious. Therefore lords all left Yi and courted Qi, saying, "Our lord is Emperor Yu's son." Thereupon Qi then assumed Son of Heaven position, this is Xia hou Emperor Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year of his reign Yu toured east to Kuaiji and died there. He left the realm to Yi. After the mourning Yi yielded to Yu's son Qi and withdrew south of Mount Ji. Yu's son Qi was worthy, and the realm's hopes settled on him. Though Yu had named Yi, Yi had served at Yu's side too briefly to win the people's trust. So the lords left Yi for Qi, saying, "He is our lord, the Son of Yu." Qi then mounted the throne as Emperor Qi of Xia.

sentences.zh: 夏后帝啟，禹之子，其母涂山氏之女也。

translations.literal: Xia hou Emperor Qi, Yu's son, his mother a woman of the Tushan clan.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Qi of Xia was Yu's son; his mother was a lady of Tushan.

translations.literal: Xia hou Emperor Qi, Yu's son, his mother a woman of the Tushan clan.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Qi of Xia was Yu's son; his mother was a lady of Tushan.

sentences.zh: 有扈氏不服，啟伐之，大戰於甘。

translations.literal: Youhu clan not submitting, Qi attacked them, great battle at Gan.
translations.idiomatic: When the Youhu refused submission, Qi attacked and fought the great battle of Gan.

sentences.zh: 將戰，作甘誓，乃召六卿申之。

translations.literal: About to battle, made Gan oath, then summoned six ministers declaring it.
translations.idiomatic: Before the fight he issued the Oath of Gan and called the six ministers to hear it.

sentences.zh: 啟曰：「嗟！

translations.literal: Qi said, "Oh!"
translations.idiomatic: Qi said, "Hear me!"

sentences.zh: 六事之人，予誓告女：有扈氏威侮五行，怠棄三正，天用勦絕其命。

translations.literal: Six affairs people, I oath tell you: Youhu clan threaten insult five phases, neglect abandon three rectifiers, Heaven uses extirpate cut off its mandate.
translations.idiomatic: You who command the six hosts, I swear this to you: Youhu has insulted the Five Agents, spurned the Three Beginnings—Heaven therefore ends their mandate.

sentences.zh: 今予維共行天之罰。

translations.literal: Now I only together conduct Heaven's punishment.
translations.idiomatic: I now join you in executing Heaven's sentence.

sentences.zh: 左不攻于左，右不攻于右，女不共命。

translations.literal: Left not attacking at left, right not attacking at right, you not together mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Those on the left who fail the left wing, those on the right who fail the right—you disobey the command.

sentences.zh: 御非其馬之政，女不共命。

translations.literal: Charioteers not their horses' regulation, you not together mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Charioteers who cannot master their teams—you likewise disobey.

sentences.zh: 用命，賞于祖；

translations.literal: Obeying mandate, rewarded at the ancestor;
translations.idiomatic: Whoever obeys will be rewarded before the ancestral shrine.

sentences.zh: 不用命，僇于社，予則帑僇女。」

translations.literal: not obeying mandate, executed at the earth altar; I will put you and your kin to death.
translations.idiomatic: Whoever disobeys will be slain at the soil altar, you and your households with you.

sentences.zh: 遂滅有扈氏。

translations.literal: Thereupon extinguished Youhu clan.
translations.idiomatic: He then destroyed the Youhu.

sentences.zh: 天下咸朝。

translations.literal: All under Heaven all courted.
translations.idiomatic: The whole realm submitted.

translations.literal: Youhu clan not submitting, Qi attacked them, great battle at Gan. About to battle, made Gan oath, then summoned six ministers declaring it. Qi said, "Oh!" Six affairs people, I oath tell you: Youhu clan threaten insult five phases, neglect abandon three rectifiers, Heaven uses extirpate cut off its mandate. Now I only together conduct Heaven's punishment. Left not attacking at left, right not attacking at right, you not together mandate. Charioteers not their horses' regulation, you not together mandate. Obeying mandate, rewarded at the ancestor; not obeying mandate, executed at the earth altar; I will put you and your kin to death. Thereupon extinguished Youhu clan. All under Heaven all courted.
translations.idiomatic: When the Youhu refused submission, Qi attacked and fought the great battle of Gan. Before the fight he issued the Oath of Gan and called the six ministers to hear it. Qi said, "Hear me!" You who command the six hosts, I swear this to you: Youhu has insulted the Five Agents, spurned the Three Beginnings—Heaven therefore ends their mandate. I now join you in executing Heaven's sentence. Those on the left who fail the left wing, those on the right who fail the right—you disobey the command. Charioteers who cannot master their teams—you likewise disobey. Whoever obeys will be rewarded before the ancestral shrine. Whoever disobeys will be slain at the soil altar, you and your households with you. He then destroyed the Youhu. The whole realm submitted.

sentences.zh: 夏后帝啟崩，子帝太康立。

translations.literal: Xia hou Emperor Qi passed away, son Emperor Taikang established.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Qi died, his son Taikang succeeded.

sentences.zh: 帝太康失國，昆弟五人，須于洛汭，作五子之歌。

translations.literal: Emperor Taikang lost state, five elder brothers, waited at Luo's bend, made Song of the Five Sons.
translations.idiomatic: Taikang lost the throne; five brothers waited at the Luo bend and composed the Song of the Five Sons.

translations.literal: Xia hou Emperor Qi passed away, son Emperor Taikang established. Emperor Taikang lost state, five elder brothers, waited at Luo's bend, made Song of the Five Sons.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Qi died, his son Taikang succeeded. Taikang lost the throne; five brothers waited at the Luo bend and composed the Song of the Five Sons.

sentences.zh: 太康崩，弟中康立，是為帝中康。

translations.literal: Taikang passed away, younger brother Zhongkang established, this is Emperor Zhongkang.
translations.idiomatic: Taikang was succeeded by his brother Zhongkang, known as Emperor Zhongkang.

sentences.zh: 帝中康時，羲、和湎淫，廢時亂日。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhongkang's time, Xi and He drowned in debauchery, abandoned seasons disordered days.
translations.idiomatic: Under Zhongkang the Xi and He stewards sank into vice and threw the calendar into confusion.

sentences.zh: 胤往征之，作胤征。

translations.literal: Yin went punishing them, made Yin expedition.
translations.idiomatic: Yin marched against them in the Punitive Expedition of Yin.

translations.literal: Taikang passed away, younger brother Zhongkang established, this is Emperor Zhongkang. Emperor Zhongkang's time, Xi and He drowned in debauchery, abandoned seasons disordered days. Yin went punishing them, made Yin expedition.
translations.idiomatic: Taikang was succeeded by his brother Zhongkang, known as Emperor Zhongkang. Under Zhongkang the Xi and He stewards sank into vice and threw the calendar into confusion. Yin marched against them in the Punitive Expedition of Yin.

sentences.zh: 中康崩，子帝相立。

translations.literal: Zhongkang passed away, son Emperor Xiang established.
translations.idiomatic: Zhongkang died and his son Xiang ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝相崩，子帝少康立。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiang passed away, son Emperor Shaokang established.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang died and his son Shaokang ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝少康崩，子帝予立。

translations.literal: Emperor Shaokang passed away, son Emperor Zhu established.
translations.idiomatic: Shaokang died and his son Zhu ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝予崩，子帝槐立。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhu passed away, son Emperor Huai established.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu died and his son Huai ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝槐崩，子帝芒立。

translations.literal: Emperor Huai passed away, son Emperor Mang established.
translations.idiomatic: Huai died and his son Mang ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝芒崩，子帝泄立。

translations.literal: Emperor Mang passed away, son Emperor Xie established.
translations.idiomatic: Mang died and his son Xie ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝泄崩，子帝不降立。

translations.literal: Emperor Xie passed away, son Emperor Bujiang established.
translations.idiomatic: Xie died and his son Bujiang ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝不降崩，弟帝扃立。

translations.literal: Emperor Bujiang passed away, younger brother Emperor Jiong established.
translations.idiomatic: Bujiang died and his brother Jiong ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝扃崩，子帝廑立。

translations.literal: Emperor Jiong passed away, son Emperor Jin established.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong died and his son Jin ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝廑崩，立帝不降之子孔甲，是為帝孔甲。

translations.literal: Emperor Jin passed away, established Emperor Bujiang's son Kongjia, this is Emperor Kongjia.
translations.idiomatic: Jin died and Bujiang's son Kongjia took the throne as Emperor Kongjia.

sentences.zh: 帝孔甲立，好方鬼神，事淫亂。

translations.literal: Emperor Kongjia established, loved methods ghosts spirits, affairs dissolute chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: Kongjia turned to occult rites and sank into disorder.

sentences.zh: 夏后氏德衰，諸侯畔之。

translations.literal: Xia hou virtue declined, lords rebelled them.
translations.idiomatic: Xia's virtue waned and the lords rebelled.

sentences.zh: 天降龍二，有雌雄，孔甲不能食，未得豢龍氏。

translations.literal: Heaven sent down dragons two, having male female, Kongjia could not feed them, not yet obtained Dragon-herd clan.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven sent down a pair of dragons, but Kongjia could not feed them and had no Dragon-keepers.

sentences.zh: 陶唐既衰，其后有劉累，學擾龍于豢龍氏，以事孔甲。

translations.literal: Taotang already declined, afterward had Liu Lei, learned tame dragons at Dragon-herd clan, to serve Kongjia.
translations.idiomatic: When the house of Taotang waned, a man named Liu Lei learned dragon-tending from the Dragon-keepers and served Kongjia.

sentences.zh: 孔甲賜之姓曰御龍氏，受豕韋之後。

translations.literal: Kongjia granted him surname said Imperial Dragon clan, received Pig-wei's posterity.
translations.idiomatic: Kongjia gave him the clan name Yinglong and the fief of the Pig-Wei line.

sentences.zh: 龍一雌死，以食夏后。

translations.literal: Dragons one female died, to feed Xia hou.
translations.idiomatic: When the female dragon died, he served its flesh to the Xia court.

sentences.zh: 夏后使求，懼而遷去。

translations.literal: Xia hou sent seek, feared then moved away.
translations.idiomatic: When Xia demanded more, he fled in terror.

translations.literal: Zhongkang passed away, son Emperor Xiang established. Emperor Xiang passed away, son Emperor Shaokang established. Emperor Shaokang passed away, son Emperor Zhu established. Emperor Zhu passed away, son Emperor Huai established. Emperor Huai passed away, son Emperor Mang established. Emperor Mang passed away, son Emperor Xie established. Emperor Xie passed away, son Emperor Bujiang established. Emperor Bujiang passed away, younger brother Emperor Jiong established. Emperor Jiong passed away, son Emperor Jin established. Emperor Jin passed away, established Emperor Bujiang's son Kongjia, this is Emperor Kongjia. Emperor Kongjia established, loved methods ghosts spirits, affairs dissolute chaotic. Xia hou virtue declined, lords rebelled them. Heaven sent down dragons two, having male female, Kongjia could not feed them, not yet obtained Dragon-herd clan. Taotang already declined, afterward had Liu Lei, learned tame dragons at Dragon-herd clan, to serve Kongjia. Kongjia granted him surname said Imperial Dragon clan, received Pig-wei's posterity. Dragons one female died, to feed Xia hou. Xia hou sent seek, feared then moved away.
translations.idiomatic: Zhongkang died and his son Xiang ruled. Xiang died and his son Shaokang ruled. Shaokang died and his son Zhu ruled. Zhu died and his son Huai ruled. Huai died and his son Mang ruled. Mang died and his son Xie ruled. Xie died and his son Bujiang ruled. Bujiang died and his brother Jiong ruled. Jiong died and his son Jin ruled. Jin died and Bujiang's son Kongjia took the throne as Emperor Kongjia. Kongjia turned to occult rites and sank into disorder. Xia's virtue waned and the lords rebelled. Heaven sent down a pair of dragons, but Kongjia could not feed them and had no Dragon-keepers. When the house of Taotang waned, a man named Liu Lei learned dragon-tending from the Dragon-keepers and served Kongjia. Kongjia gave him the clan name Yinglong and the fief of the Pig-Wei line. When the female dragon died, he served its flesh to the Xia court. When Xia demanded more, he fled in terror.

sentences.zh: 孔甲崩，子帝皋立。

translations.literal: Kongjia passed away, son Emperor Gao established.
translations.idiomatic: Kongjia died and his son Gao ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝皋崩，子帝發立。

translations.literal: Emperor Gao passed away, son Emperor Fa established.
translations.idiomatic: Gao died and his son Fa ruled.

sentences.zh: 帝發崩，子帝履癸立，是為桀。

translations.literal: Emperor Fa passed away, son Emperor Lügui established, this is Jie.
translations.idiomatic: Fa died and his son Lügui became the tyrant Jie.

sentences.zh: 帝桀之時，自孔甲以來而諸侯多畔夏，桀不務德而武傷百姓，百姓弗堪。

translations.literal: Emperor Jie's time, from Kongjia onward many lords rebelled Xia, Jie not pursuing virtue but martially injuring hundred surnames, hundred surnames not endure.
translations.idiomatic: Under Jie, lords had rebelled since Kongjia's day; Jie cultivated force, not virtue, until the people could bear no more.

sentences.zh: 乃召湯而囚之夏臺，已而釋之。

translations.literal: Then summoned Tang and imprisoned him at Xia tower, afterward released him.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned Tang, jailed him in the Xia tower, then let him go.

sentences.zh: 湯修德，諸侯皆歸湯，湯遂率兵以伐夏桀。

translations.literal: Tang cultivated virtue, lords all returned Tang, Tang thereupon led troops to attack Xia Jie.
translations.idiomatic: Tang built up his virtue until the lords rallied to him, then led an army against Jie.

sentences.zh: 桀走鳴條，遂放而死。

translations.literal: Jie fled Mingtiao, thereupon banished then died.
translations.idiomatic: Jie fled to Mingtiao, was driven out, and died.

sentences.zh: 桀謂人曰：「吾悔不遂殺湯於夏臺，使至此。」

translations.literal: Jie told people saying, "I regret not then killing Tang at Xia tower, causing arrive here."
translations.idiomatic: Jie said to those about him, "I rue not killing Tang in the Xia tower when I could—now I am brought to this pass."

sentences.zh: 湯乃踐天子位，代夏朝天下。

translations.literal: Tang thereupon trod Son of Heaven position, replacing Xia facing all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Tang then took the throne in Xia's place and faced the realm as Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 湯封夏之後，至周封於杞也。

translations.literal: Tang enfeoffed Xia's posterity, reaching Zhou enfeoffed at Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Tang enfeoffed Xia's descendants; by Zhou times they held the state of Qi.

translations.literal: Kongjia passed away, son Emperor Gao established. Emperor Gao passed away, son Emperor Fa established. Emperor Fa passed away, son Emperor Lügui established, this is Jie. Emperor Jie's time, from Kongjia onward many lords rebelled Xia, Jie not pursuing virtue but martially injuring hundred surnames, hundred surnames not endure. Then summoned Tang and imprisoned him at Xia tower, afterward released him. Tang cultivated virtue, lords all returned Tang, Tang thereupon led troops to attack Xia Jie. Jie fled Mingtiao, thereupon banished then died. Jie told people saying, "I regret not then killing Tang at Xia tower, causing arrive here." Tang thereupon trod Son of Heaven position, replacing Xia facing all under Heaven. Tang enfeoffed Xia's posterity, reaching Zhou enfeoffed at Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Kongjia died and his son Gao ruled. Gao died and his son Fa ruled. Fa died and his son Lügui became the tyrant Jie. Under Jie, lords had rebelled since Kongjia's day; Jie cultivated force, not virtue, until the people could bear no more. He summoned Tang, jailed him in the Xia tower, then let him go. Tang built up his virtue until the lords rallied to him, then led an army against Jie. Jie fled to Mingtiao, was driven out, and died. Jie said to those about him, "I rue not killing Tang in the Xia tower when I could—now I am brought to this pass." Tang then took the throne in Xia's place and faced the realm as Son of Heaven. Tang enfeoffed Xia's descendants; by Zhou times they held the state of Qi.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：禹為姒姓，其後分封，用國為姓，故有夏后氏、有扈氏、有男氏、斟尋氏、彤城氏、褒氏、費 氏、杞氏、繒氏、辛氏、冥氏、斟（氏）戈氏。

translations.literal: The Grand Scribe says: Yu bore the Si surname; afterward divided enfeoffments, used states as surnames, therefore there were Xia hou, Youhu, Younan, Zhenxun, Tongcheng, Bao, Fei, Qi, Zeng, Xin, Ming, Zhen, Ge clans.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Scribe remarks: Yu's line took the name Si; later branches adopted their states as surnames, producing houses such as Xiahou, Youhu, Younan, Zhenxun, Tongcheng, Bao, Fei, Qi, Zeng, Xin, Ming, Zhen, and Ge.

sentences.zh: 孔子正夏時，學者多傳夏小正云。

translations.literal: Confucius corrected Xia calendar, scholars many transmit Xia minor calendar saying.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius set the Xia calendar straight, and scholars still pass down the Xia minor calendar.

sentences.zh: 自虞、夏時，貢賦備矣。

translations.literal: From Yu and Xia time, tribute levies already complete.
translations.idiomatic: From the age of Yu and Xia the tribute system was already full-formed.

sentences.zh: 或言禹會諸侯江南，計功而崩，因葬焉，命曰會稽。

translations.literal: Some say Yu met lords south of the river, reckoned merit then passed away, thereby buried there, commanded called Kuaiji.
translations.idiomatic: Some hold that Yu met the lords south of the Yangtze, settled accounts of merit, and died there—hence the name Kuaiji, "gathering of tallies."

sentences.zh: 會稽者，會計也。

translations.literal: Kuaiji means reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: Kuaiji means the reckoning itself.
